summary: Back at a World Cup for the first time since 1998 with a genuine golden generation. Under Ståle Solbakken they outscored everyone in qualifying (37 goals), scoring in flurries while becoming more solid defensively. Built around Erling Haaland — and deadly on the counter.
style: A lop-sided 4-3-3 with Sørloth as a wide forward off the right (hard-running Ryerson behind him). Super strengths are wide crosses and counter-attacks: dribbly wingers Nusa and Schjelderup whip inswingers, left-back Wolfe supplies the final ball, and Sørloth + Haaland give two aerial targets. Build varies — short from Nyland or long to Sørloth with midfielders on the second balls — and Ødegaard threads through balls for Haaland. Defends in a 4-1-4-1 mid-block so Haaland conserves energy.

counterReliance.note: Mid-block then lethal counters — most counter goals in European qualifying

strengths: Two elite aerial/box threats (Haaland, Sørloth) fed by high-quality crossing
Devastating in transition — seven counter-attack goals in qualifying, the most in Europe
Ødegaard's killer passing, especially through balls for Haaland
A peak-years core and a prolific, in-flurries attack (37 goals in qualifying)
weaknesses: Struggles against a high press — the defensive line is too passive to press-breaking passes over midfield

keyPlayers.name: Erling Haaland
keyPlayers.note: The team is built around him — the finisher at the end of the crosses and counters

keyPlayers.name: Martin Ødegaard
keyPlayers.note: Creator-in-chief — pulls wide to release Haaland or Ryerson; top assister in European qualifying (7)

keyPlayers.name: Julian Ryerson
keyPlayers.note: Under-the-radar menace — 15 assists this season; lethal on the overlap with a long throw too

tactics: Sit in a 4-1-4-1 to conserve Haaland, then spring counters and whip crosses to the big men; let Ødegaard pick the lock. The way to trouble them is a sustained high press over the top of their passive line.
notes: First major tournament since Euro 2000; Solbakken played in that one.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group I (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
